title: rubus hylocharis
intro: Rubus hylocharis, also known as the mountain blackberry, is a species of bramble in the rose family that is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The health benefits of Rubus hylocharis include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Rubus hylocharis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and has been used in herbal medicine to support overall health and well-being.
